title: Source IP should be 127.0.0.1 in rootless Podman 1.4.4 - RHEL 7.7
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

For a rootless container the source IP of incoming packets on a publish port is always `10.0.2.2`.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Start a NGINX container:
`podman run -p 8888:80 docker.io/library/nginx:latest`

2. Make a request from another terminal:
`curl http://localhost:8888`

3. Look at the source ip of the request in NGINX stdout log:
`10.0.2.2 - - [19/Jun/2020:15:29:21 +0000] "GET / HTTP/1.1" 200 612 "-" "curl/7.29.0" "-"`

**Describe the results you received:**

The logged source address is always 10.0.2.2

**Describe the results you expected:**

The logged source IP address to match the IP of the host the request was coming from. In this case, 127.0.0.1. This is especially important here as I am setting up PosgreSQL and its security with IP based ACL.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.4.4
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.10.3
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.10.3
  podman version: 1.4.4
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.9.0
  Conmon:
    package: podman-1.4.4-4.el7.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 0.3.0, commit: unknown'
  Distribution:
    distribution: '"rhel"'
    version: "7.7"
  MemFree: 8009859072
  MemTotal: 16656347136
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-65.rc8.el7.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 8589930496
  SwapTotal: 8589930496
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 8
  hostname: removed
  kernel: 3.10.0-1062.9.1.el7.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  uptime: 646h 18m 3.92s (Approximately 26.92 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ConfigFile: edited/.config/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 2
  GraphDriverName: vfs
  GraphOptions: null
  GraphRoot: edited/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus: {}
  ImageStore:
    number: 3
  RunRoot: /run/user/6661
  VolumePath: edited/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.4.4-4.el7.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

NAME="Red Hat Enterprise Linux Server"
VERSION="7.7 (Maipo)"


Do you have a workaround possible within version 1.4.4 or at least if I need to recompile, to be doable on CentOS 7.7?

Thx a lot for your valuable work

comments.body:
I see slirp is ran with this command `/usr/bin/slirp4netns --api-socket /run/user/6661/libpod/tmp/fa9929cebaf1f0a21416d2b386375353576c81882dae79a55cc92d272eac1bd8.net 18920 --disable-host-loopback --mtu 65520 -c -e 3 -r 4 18920 tap0`

Not sure if this helps to understand the context here.

Hmmm. This is 1.4.4, so pre-Rootlesskit - and I was under the impression that slirp did preserve source IP?

@giuseppe @AkihiroSuda Any ideas here?

It does keep the source IP if it is not localhost. But for localhost, it transforms it to 10.0.2.2. Is this because of the parameter `--disable-host-loopback` ? If yes, any ways to tell podman to not use the parameter ? If you have pointers to some blogs/docs ? Is only doc the source of slirp4netns ? Thx
Please update to a newer version of podman,  podman 1.6.4 should be the latest in RHEL7.
